data.title: Ecora: Strengthened Balance Sheet and Copper-Focused Royalty Growth Support Buy Rating and C$4.00 Target
data.description:
Ecora Royalties PLC, the Basic Materials sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst on March 26. Analyst Nicholas Cortellucci from Atrium Research maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a C$4.00 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nicholas Cortellucci has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily Ecora’s successful transition toward critical minerals, with copper now at the core of its portfolio. In 2025 the business delivered materially higher operating and free cash flow, reflecting the strength and resilience of its royalty assets and confirming that the new commodity mix is already translating into stronger financial performance.

He also emphasizes the company’s significantly reduced leverage, which enhances balance sheet strength and leaves ample room to fund additional royalty acquisitions alongside its ongoing organic growth projects. With several key assets ramping up, a solid development pipeline, and enhanced flexibility to pursue accretive deals, Cortellucci sees a clear pathway to meaningful, copper‑focused growth, supporting his C$4.00 per‑share target and Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Cortellucci is an analyst with an average return of -6.4% and a 30.97% success rate.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – PerPlexity also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a p140.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Advancing Azenosertib Pipeline and DENALI Readouts Underpin Buy Rating on Zentalis Pharmaceuticals
data.description:
Michael Schmidt, an analyst from Guggenheim, maintained the Buy rating on Zentalis Pharmaceuticals. The associated price target is $6.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Michael Schmidt has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the advancing clinical profile of azenosertib in ovarian cancer and the increasing clarity on the DENALI Part 2 program. The upcoming dose selection in 1H26 and the plan to enroll approximately 70 patients at the chosen dose create a defined path toward topline data that management believes could support an accelerated approval by year-end 2026.

The initiation of the MUIR Phase 1b combination trial in ovarian cancer and the planned ASPENOVA Phase 3 confirmatory study, both progressing on schedule, further strengthen the long-term value proposition. Together, these catalysts, along with a structured development strategy across Phase 1b, Part 2 of DENALI, and the Phase 3 program, underpin Schmidt’s favorable risk‑reward view on Zentalis Pharmaceuticals and justify his Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
